name: sec_edgar_filings_scraper
title: SEC EDGAR Filings Scraper
description: Fetch recent SEC EDGAR company filings or one XBRL company concept by ticker or CIK through the SEC's public JSON APIs, returning normalized company metadata, filing URLs, forms, dates, accession numbers, and fact rows.

seo.title: SEC EDGAR Filings Scraper Tool for Company Filings and XBRL Facts
seo.description: Fetch recent SEC EDGAR filings or XBRL company concept facts by ticker or CIK with Better Fetch, including accession numbers, form types, filing/report dates, primary documents, filing URLs, and normalized fact rows.
seo.intro: SEC EDGAR Filings Scraper gives agents a bounded, compliant route into the SEC's public EDGAR JSON APIs. Resolve a public company ticker to CIK, return recent submissions from the SEC submissions endpoint, filter by forms such as 10-K, 10-Q, and 8-K, and build direct filing and filing-detail URLs. In company-concept mode, fetch one XBRL taxonomy concept such as us-gaap/Assets and return recent fact rows by unit, fiscal year, fiscal period, form, filing date, frame, accession number, and value. It is designed for public-company monitoring, earnings workflows, financial research, disclosure alerts, RAG ingestion, diligence packets, and agent-readable SEC filing context.

useCases.title: Filing monitors
useCases.description: Watch recent 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, S-1, or proxy filings for a ticker and return direct SEC filing URLs plus accession metadata.

useCases.title: Financial research
useCases.description: Pull one XBRL concept such as Assets or EntityCommonStockSharesOutstanding into a normalized fact table for models and notebooks.

useCases.title: Agent diligence packets
useCases.description: Give an agent clean company metadata, filing dates, report periods, primary document URLs, and recent fact rows before deeper reading.

faqs.question: Does SEC EDGAR Filings Scraper require an API key?
faqs.answer: No. Version 0.1 uses the SEC's public JSON APIs with an identifying user agent. SEC fair-access rules still apply, including the SEC's documented request-rate expectations.

faqs.question: Does it parse full filing text or exhibits?
faqs.answer: No. It returns normalized submission metadata, filing URLs, and selected XBRL concept facts. Full filing text extraction, exhibits, tables, and multi-page historical archives are future work.

faqs.question: Is this tool affiliated with the SEC?
faqs.answer: No. Better Fetch is not affiliated with or endorsed by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. It uses public SEC data surfaces.

mode.description: filings = recent EDGAR submissions; company_concept = one XBRL concept from the SEC companyconcept API.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol such as AAPL. Used when cik is not supplied.

cik.description: SEC CIK, with or without leading zeros.

forms.description: Comma-separated form filter for filings mode, such as 10-K,10-Q,8-K.

taxonomy.description: XBRL taxonomy for company_concept mode, usually us-gaap or dei.

concept.description: XBRL concept tag for company_concept mode, such as Assets.

unit.description: Optional unit filter for company_concept mode, such as USD or shares.

limit.description: Maximum filing or fact rows to return.

source_url.description: SEC JSON URL fetched for the primary request

count.description: Number of filing or fact rows returned
